content:
$1,530  (45 hrs) $2,040 (60 hrs)

 
  1. Key Takeaways & Questions on Compensation Structure
 
·         St. Paul’s compensates adjuncts at a significantly lower rate than comparable institutions, while also requiring strict hourly time-tracking.
·         At CUNY, Rutgers, and NYIT, adjunct pay accounts for grading, course prep, student engagement, and administrative work as part of a per-credit or per-course rate.
·         St. Paul’s model, in contrast, only compensates direct classroom hours, requiring faculty to clock in and out.
 
This raises critical questions:
1. How does St. Paul’s define adjunct workload expectations beyond in-class hours?
2. Are grading, curriculum development, and student engagement expected to be unpaid labor?
3. If these responsibilities should be compensated, what is the proper procedure for logging these hours?
context: From document: Dear Dr. Guirguis, — chunk 15 (chunk 15/16)
